title: 2026 U.S. Senate election in Arkansas
description: Voters in Arkansas will elect one member to the U.S. Senate in the general election on November 3, 2026. The seat is currently held by Republican Tom Cotton, who first took office in 2015 and is seeking another term. Arkansas is a strongly Republican state, so the race is expected to favor the GOP, but the contest still matters because it will determine who represents the state in the Senate and help shape the chamber’s partisan balance. The general election is likely to feature Cotton against the Democratic nominee, with the outcome reflecting both Cotton’s incumbency and the state’s partisan lean.
polling_note: No public polling found for this race as of 2026-04-06.

candidates.name: Tom Cotton
candidates.summary: Tom Cotton is the incumbent U.S. senator from Arkansas and has served since 2015. According to his Senate biography, he grew up on a cattle farm in Yell County, graduated from Harvard and Harvard Law School, served nearly five years as an Army infantry officer with deployments to Iraq and Afghanistan, and later served one term in the U.S. House before returning to the Senate.

career_history.title: Member of Congress
career_history.description: Served one term in the House before being elected to the Senate.

career_history.title: Infantry Officer
career_history.description: Served nearly five years on active duty, including deployments to Iraq and Afghanistan.

candidates.donor_summary: According to OpenSecrets, Cotton's campaign committee fundraising is reported separately from his leadership PAC. The OpenSecrets profile shows his leadership PAC, Republican Majority Fund, raised $16,312,092 and had $7,450,869 cash on hand as of the latest report, while the top disclosed contributors to his campaign committee include Apollo Global Management, Stephens Inc., McNa Dental Plans, Pro-Israel America PAC, and Blackstone Group.
candidates.voting_summary: Tom Cotton is an incumbent U.S. senator, so he has a public congressional voting record. GovTrack identifies him as serving since 2015, and Vote Smart lists his key votes; his record is consistently aligned with the Republican caucus, including votes on appropriations and other major Senate roll calls. Notable recent examples include a Nay vote on the continuing appropriations and extensions act for 2026 and other Senate roll-call votes documented by Congress.gov/Senate records.

links.title: Sen. Tom Cotton - Campaign Finance Summary - OpenSecrets
Sen. Tom Cotton [R-AR, 2015-2026], Senator for Arkansas - GovTrack
Official U.S. Senate website for Tom Cotton

Healthcare.stance: Tom Cotton supports protections for people with pre-existing conditions and has backed legislation to guarantee coverage and prohibit discrimination based on health status. He has also supported repealing Obamacare and pursuing market-based health care reforms.

sources.title: Senators Introduce the Protect Act to Ensure Protections and Affordable Coverage for Americans with Pre-Existing Conditions
Cotton Statement on the Senate's Vote to Repeal Obamacare

Economy.stance: Tom Cotton supports a pro-growth economic approach that emphasizes lower costs, deregulation, and tougher trade policy toward China. He has also promoted legislation aimed at lowering energy costs for Arkansans and expanding energy innovation.

sources.title: Cotton Reintroduces Legislation to End Permanent Normal Trade Relations with China
Cotton Introduces Bill to Lower Energy Costs for Arkansans

Climate/Energy.stance: Tom Cotton emphasizes affordable and reliable energy and has opposed policies he says would raise electricity costs. He has introduced legislation to limit state climate mandates and to lower Arkansas energy prices.

sources.title: Cotton Introduces Bill to Lower Arkansas Energy Prices
Cotton Introduces Bill to Lower Energy Costs for Arkansans

Reproductive Rights.stance: Tom Cotton is strongly anti-abortion and supports restricting abortion access. He has defended unborn life and backed federal abortion restrictions.

sources.title: Cotton Defends the Right to Life - Senate Website
Tom Cotton Congress.gov profile

Immigration.stance: Tom Cotton supports strict immigration enforcement, including mandatory E-Verify and stronger border controls. He has also backed legislation to limit asylum access and reduce illegal immigration.

sources.title: Cotton, Boozman Introduce National, Mandatory E-Verify Legislation
Cotton Introduces Bill to Solve the Border Crisis

Guns & Safety.stance: Tom Cotton opposes major federal gun-control measures and supports Second Amendment rights. He has backed efforts to repeal the tax on certain firearm purchases and to reduce restrictions on suppressors, while emphasizing tougher penalties for violent criminals.

sources.title: Cotton Statement on Senate Gun-Control Legislation
Cotton, Colleagues Reintroduce Bill to Repeal Tax on Certain Firearm Purchases (RIFLE Act)
Cotton, Boozman, Crapo Introduce Hearing Protection Act

Foreign Policy.stance: Tom Cotton advocates a hardline foreign policy focused on military strength and countering adversaries such as China. He has opposed the Iran nuclear deal and supported measures to restrict foreign influence in U.S. research and national security.

sources.title: Cotton Reintroduces Legislation to End Permanent Normal Trade Relations with China
Cotton Senators to Wright Ban Chinese Nationals from American Laboratories
A Conversation on the Iran Nuclear Deal With Senator Tom Cotton

Social Justice.stance: Tom Cotton opposes race-based DEI initiatives and argues for color-blind, merit-based policies in hiring and governance. He has also criticized cancel culture and other social-justice activism.

sources.title: Cotton Warns Top Law Firms About Race-Based Hiring Practices
Cotton Takes on Cancel Culture

Education.stance: Tom Cotton supports school choice, workforce education, and tighter scrutiny of foreign influence in higher education. He has also backed legislation to study cellphone use in schools and to protect sensitive academic research.

sources.title: Cotton Introduces Bill to Put Arkansas Students First
Cotton Introduces Bill to Overhaul Workforce Education
Cotton, Kaine Reintroduce Bill to Study Effects of Cellphones in Schools

Tech & AI.stance: Tom Cotton supports U.S. leadership in AI and robotics while limiting access by foreign adversaries. He has introduced measures to protect Americans’ data and to preserve American AI dominance.

sources.title: Cotton, Schumer Introduce Bipartisan Bill to Protect Americans’ Data from Foreign Adversaries
Cotton to Lutnick: Now is the Time to Preserve American AI Dominance

Election Reform.stance: Tom Cotton opposes federal election reform legislation such as the For the People Act, arguing that elections should remain primarily under state control. He has also supported voter ID requirements as a safeguard.

sources.title: The 'For the People Act' Has Nothing to Offer Our Country
Senate to vote on S1 For the People Act bill - CNBC

Local Issues.stance: Tom Cotton has promoted the DATA Act of 2026, which would exempt certain off-grid electricity providers and consumer-regulated utilities from some federal rules to support customized power systems for data centers and other energy-intensive users. He says the proposal would lower energy costs and encourage innovation in Arkansas.

sources.title: Cotton Introduces Bill to Lower Energy Costs for Arkansans
DATA Act of 2026 - S.3585 text

candidates.name: Hallie Shoffner
candidates.summary: Hallie Shoffner is a Democratic candidate for U.S. Senate in Arkansas and a sixth-generation farmer from Newport. Her campaign says she previously operated her family farm and has worked in conservation, food systems, and public service, and she is running on an Arkansas-focused message centered on working families, farmers, and rural communities.

summary_sources.title: Shoffner Selected to Presidential Leadership Scholars

career_history.title: Farmer and small-business owner
career_history.description: Operated the family farm in Arkansas before it closed in 2024.

career_history.title: Conservation and food systems advocate
career_history.description: Worked on conservation, sustainable farming, regional food production, and hunger relief.

candidates.donor_summary: Hallie Shoffner's principal campaign committee has raised about $1.24 million in the 2025–2026 cycle, with nearly all receipts coming from individual contributions and only a small amount from other committees. The committee reported about $503,970 in cash on hand and no candidate loans; the available FEC summary does not show a dominant PAC or industry source.
candidates.voting_summary: Hallie Shoffner has not held legislative office, so there is no legislative voting record to report. Public evidence of her governing approach comes from campaign statements and issue messaging as a farmer and candidate, especially on agriculture and tariffs; she has criticized tariffs as harmful to Arkansas farmers and framed her campaign around rural economic policy.

links.title: 2026 United States Senate election in Arkansas - Wikipedia
HALLIE SHOFFNER FOR ARKANSAS - committee overview - FEC
OPINION | HALLIE SHOFFNER: Tariffs can work when used as a tool, not a sledgehammer
Arkansas farmer challenges Cotton for U.S. Senate seat

Healthcare.stance: She will fight to make healthcare affordable and accessible by protecting hospitals and lowering prescription drug costs. She also pledges to strengthen the Affordable Care Act, protect Medicare and Medicaid, cut red tape, and hold insurance companies accountable.

sources.title: Hallie Shoffner's Priorities

Economy.stance: Hallie Shoffner advocates an Arkansas-first economic plan aimed at lowering the cost of living for working families, expanding local jobs, and supporting farmers. She calls for raising the minimum wage, expanding workforce training, cutting taxes for working families, reducing red tape for businesses, and investing in infrastructure and education.

sources.title: Hallie’s Priorities
The Issues

Climate/Energy.stance: Hallie Shoffner supports climate resilience for Arkansas agriculture and rural communities, advocating for policies to help farmers adapt to climate change, including crop diversification and water-use efficiency, and a Farm Bill that strengthens risk management and supports conservation and sustainable farming.

sources.title: Hallie Shoffner priorities - A Plan That Puts Arkansas Families First
Meet the Arkansas farmer trying to adapt to the changing climate

Reproductive Rights.stance: Hallie Shoffner's campaign frames reproductive rights as part of broader maternal health, advocating for affordable, accessible healthcare for women and families and addressing maternal mortality to ensure lifesaving medical care is available when needed.

sources.title: Hallie Shoffner's Priorities

Immigration.stance: Shoffner says the immigration system is broken and supports immigration reform, but she has not laid out a detailed policy platform on enforcement or legal immigration changes in the campaign materials reviewed. In an Arkansas Advocate interview, she said federal agents’ actions were further dividing the nation and that both parties should come together on a real solution.

sources.title: Issue Spotlight: Where Arkansas’ U.S. Senate candidates stand on immigration policy
Hallie's Priorities

Guns & Safety.stance: No public position found on gun policy in the campaign materials reviewed. Her campaign does say she wants to keep Arkansas communities safe by supporting local law enforcement and first responders, investing in community-based crime prevention, and addressing domestic violence, human trafficking, and crimes against children.

sources.title: Hallie's Priorities

Foreign Policy.stance: No public position found on broader foreign policy issues in the campaign materials reviewed. The campaign materials and interviews located focus mainly on Arkansas economic issues, agriculture, healthcare, and public safety.

sources.title: Hallie's Priorities
Sen. Tom Cotton, Arkansas candidates for U.S. Senate react to U.S. attacks on Iran

Social Justice.stance: Hallie Shoffner frames social justice around fairness for working Arkansans, arguing that the system is rigged against everyday families. She supports strengthening social safety nets (Social Security and Medicare), making healthcare affordable, expanding education and workforce training, and empowering workers through stronger unions to ensure dignity and opportunity for all.

sources.title: Hallie Shoffner priorities
The Issues

Education.stance: Hallie Shoffner supports significantly increasing education funding by paying teachers and school staff what they deserve, while also investing in workforce and technical education, apprenticeships, and partnerships with community colleges to prepare students for good-paying jobs.

sources.title: Hallie's Priorities

Tech & AI.stance: Hallie Shoffner says data centers and AI can be a positive part of Arkansas's economy if energy costs and the grid impacts are managed, but she opposes deregulation like Cotton's DATA Act that would primarily benefit data-center developers at the expense of ratepayers and rural communities.

sources.title: Shoffner blasts Cotton over his DATA Act to deregulate energy for data centers
Arkansas politicians weigh in on data center development, mindful of impact on energy costs

Election Reform.stance: No public position found on election reform or voting-access policy in the campaign materials reviewed. The campaign's published priorities instead emphasize costs, healthcare, education, workers, farmers, and public safety.

sources.title: Hallie's Priorities
The Issues
Hallie Shoffner

Local Issues.stance: Shoffner endorses an Arkansas-first economic plan focused on lowering living costs for working families, expanding local jobs, supporting unions, and investing in education and infrastructure. She also commits to protecting Social Security and Medicare, expanding healthcare access, aiding farmers and rural communities, and reducing hunger.

sources.title: Hallie Shoffner – Priorities

candidates.name: Jeff Wadlin
candidates.summary: Jeff Wadlin is the Libertarian nominee for U.S. Senate in Arkansas and is on the general-election ballot in November 2026. Ballotpedia says he advanced from the Libertarian Party convention on February 22, 2026.

summary_sources.title: United States Senate election in Arkansas, 2026 - Ballotpedia

career_history.title: Candidate for Justice of the Peace, District 8
career_history.description: Ran as a Libertarian candidate for Benton County Quorum Court District 8 in the 2018 general election.

candidates.donor_summary: No campaign finance data found in public disclosures as of 2026-04-06. The FEC committee search and public finance pages surfaced no clearly usable federal committee totals for Wadlin, and no OpenSecrets candidate finance page was identified in the available results.
candidates.voting_summary: Jeff Wadlin has not held legislative office, so there is no legislative voting record to report. Publicly available evidence of his policy approach is limited to campaign messaging and his official website, where he says he is running to unify Arkansans and increase their power at the federal level.

flags.concern: The profile states Cotton 'served nearly five years on active duty,' with start_year 2005 and end_year 2009 — that span is approximately four years, not 'nearly five years.' The official Senate biography language ('nearly five years') is cited as the source, so if the biography uses that phrasing it may be correct, but the dates 2005–2009 are internally in tension with the description. A human reviewer should confirm whether the official biography says 'nearly five years' or a different duration, and whether the start/end years are accurate.
flags.suggestion: Check the official Senate biography at https://www.cotton.senate.gov/about to confirm the exact phrasing and service dates, and align the career_history dates with whatever the biography states.

flags.concern: The source title reads 'Cotton Senators to Wright Ban Chinese Nationals from American Laboratories' — 'to Wright' appears to be a garbled or OCR-corrupted rendering of 'to Write' or possibly a different word. This may indicate a data-extraction error in the title field.
flags.suggestion: Verify the actual press release title at the linked URL (https://www.cotton.senate.gov/news/press-releases/cotton-senators-to-wright-ban-chinese-nationals-from-american-laboratories) and correct the title to match the page.

flags.concern: The links array mixes raw URL strings (e.g., 'https://www.cotton.senate.gov/about', 'https://ballotpedia.org/Tom_Cotton', 'https://www.fec.gov/data/elections/senate/AR/2026/') with structured objects containing url/title/type fields. The same pattern exists for candidates[1] and candidates[2]. This inconsistent format may cause downstream rendering or parsing errors.
flags.suggestion: Normalize all entries in the links arrays to use the same structured object format {url, title, type} throughout, removing bare string entries.

flags.concern: The donor summary states Cotton's Republican Majority Fund raised '$16,312,092' and had '$7,450,869 cash on hand.' These figures are cited to OpenSecrets, which is a credible source, but no date or reporting period is specified for these figures. Without a reporting period, readers cannot assess how current the data is.
flags.suggestion: Add the reporting period or filing date for these figures (e.g., 'as of the [cycle/date] filing') to give readers context for the fundraising totals.

flags.concern: The profile states Shoffner's family farm 'closed in 2024' (end_year: 2024). This specific detail — that the farm closed rather than that she transitioned away from it — is a meaningful claim that should be verified against the cited campaign source. If the farm closed due to financial difficulty, that is a politically significant biographical fact that the campaign may frame differently.
flags.suggestion: Verify the specific 'closed in 2024' claim against the official campaign biography at https://www.hallieshoffner.com/about to confirm the language and framing are accurate and not editorially added.

flags.concern: The stance is rated medium confidence and frames Shoffner's position primarily around maternal health without explicitly stating her position on abortion rights or access. For a 2026 Senate candidate, this may underrepresent her public stance if she has made clearer statements elsewhere. The source cited is only her campaign priorities page.
flags.suggestion: Search for any direct statements by Shoffner on abortion rights in interviews (e.g., Arkansas Advocate, UALR Public Radio) and update the stance with more specific language if available, or note that no direct abortion-access position was found in public statements.

flags.concern: All 12 issue stances for Jeff Wadlin are 'No public position found' with low confidence, each citing only the Ballotpedia page. While this is acceptable for an obscure Libertarian candidate, the profile does not reflect any attempt to check Wadlin's official website (https://www.jeffwadlin.com/) for issue positions, even though that URL is listed in the links and the voting_source_url.
flags.suggestion: Attempt to retrieve issue positions from https://www.jeffwadlin.com/ and update stances accordingly, or explicitly note in each stance that the official website was checked and contained no policy detail, to demonstrate good-faith research effort.

flags.concern: The Local Issues stance for Shoffner duplicates much of the Economy stance content (lowering living costs, expanding local jobs, supporting unions, investing in education and infrastructure). There is significant overlap that may not add meaningful differentiation between these two issue categories.
flags.suggestion: Consider whether the Local Issues stance should be more specifically focused on Arkansas-specific local concerns (e.g., rural broadband, agricultural policy, specific state infrastructure) rather than restating the general economic platform.

flags.concern: The election date is listed as 2026-11-03 (Tuesday, November 3, 2026). November 3, 2026 is indeed a Tuesday, which is consistent with federal election law requiring elections on the first Tuesday after the first Monday in November. In 2026, the first Monday in November is November 2, making November 3 the correct general election date. This appears correct.

The Local Issues stance references the 'DATA Act of 2026' and cites S.3585. The bill citation is specific and credible, and the Congress.gov URL is provided. However, calling it the 'DATA Act of 2026' in the section title may be a branded name that differs from the official bill title. Human reviewer should confirm the official short title of S.3585 matches 'DATA Act of 2026.'
flags.suggestion: Verify the official short title of S.3585 at the Congress.gov link and ensure the name used in the stance matches the bill's official title.

reviews.summary: The profile is broadly well-constructed with strong sourcing for Tom Cotton (official Senate press releases, GovTrack, OpenSecrets, Ballotpedia, Congress.gov) and solid sourcing for Hallie Shoffner (official campaign site, FEC, credible Arkansas news outlets). Issue stances are well-documented and confidence levels are appropriately calibrated. However, several flags warrant attention: a minor structural inconsistency in the links array (mixing raw strings with objects), a potentially misleading claim about Cotton's Army service duration, one title in the Foreign Policy source for Cotton that appears garbled, and a metadata note about model names in the generator array that are unfamiliar but are correctly treated as pipeline metadata.

flags.concern: The sources are labeled as 'campaign press release' but the URLs point to 'cotton.senate.gov', which are official Senate press releases. This is a minor categorization mismatch.
flags.suggestion: Update 'type' to 'official press release' to reflect the domain.

flags.concern: Stance is marked with 'medium' confidence. While the description is accurately derived from her priorities page regarding maternal health, my training data suggests Democratic candidates in this cycle often have more explicit 'pro-choice' messaging that might be found in social media or later campaign stops not captured here.
flags.suggestion: Human reviewer should monitor for more explicit statements on the Women's Health Protection Act or similar legislation as the campaign progresses.

reviews.summary: This profile is exceptionally high-quality, demonstrating a sophisticated use of live web sources to provide a current and detailed snapshot of the 2026 Arkansas Senate race. The agent successfully retrieved specific legislative data (e.g., the DATA Act of 2026, S.3585) and used a diverse range of sources, including official Senate records, FEC filings, and local Arkansas news outlets. The distinction between Tom Cotton’s leadership PAC and campaign committee fundraising shows high attention to detail, and the coverage of the challenger Hallie Shoffner is comprehensive, especially regarding her specific policy disagreements with the incumbent.

flags.concern: Jeff Wadlin's issue stances are largely marked as 'no public position found' with low confidence. While this is acceptable given the cited search effort on Ballotpedia and the candidate's limited public profile, additional sources (if available) could enhance completeness.
flags.suggestion: If possible, check for any recent statements or social media posts by the candidate for updated positions; otherwise, maintain the current 'no public position found' notation.

flags.concern: Hallie Shoffner's stance on reproductive rights is framed indirectly as part of maternal health, with medium confidence. The cited source supports this framing, but the position could be clearer on specific policies regarding abortion access or restrictions.
flags.suggestion: If additional campaign statements or interviews are available, include them to clarify the candidate’s direct stance on reproductive rights policies.

flags.concern: Hallie Shoffner's stance on immigration is noted with medium confidence and lacks detailed policy specifics in the cited materials. The sources provided support the general position, but the stance could benefit from more granular detail if available.
flags.suggestion: Search for any further campaign statements or policy papers that might elaborate on specific immigration reform proposals.

flags.concern: Tom Cotton's voting summary mentions a 'Nay vote on the continuing appropriations and extensions act for 2026,' but the source cited (GovTrack) does not explicitly confirm this specific vote as of the profile's update date. Given the future date of the election, this may refer to a projected or anticipated vote.
flags.suggestion: Verify the specific vote reference against Congress.gov or other legislative records if available, or clarify that this is based on a pattern of past voting behavior if the vote has not yet occurred.

reviews.summary: This profile for the 2026 U.S. Senate election in Arkansas is exceptionally well-researched and comprehensive. It provides detailed, well-sourced information on all three candidates—Tom Cotton, Hallie Shoffner, and Jeff Wadlin—with high-quality citations from official campaign sites, legislative records, FEC data, and credible news outlets. The issue stances for Cotton and Shoffner are thoroughly documented with high-confidence sources, and the lack of public positions for Wadlin is appropriately noted with evidence of a good-faith search. The language remains neutral and nonpartisan throughout, and candidate backgrounds are consistent with cited sources. Minor gaps in Wadlin’s issue positions do not detract from the overall quality, as they reflect the candidate’s limited public profile rather than a research failure.

validation_grade.summary: Validated by 2/3 reviewers with an average score of 90/100.
